purpose: A data provider module that exposes a CRUD REST API over an in-memory key-value store with TTL support. Provides structured data access for downstream modules without requiring external database dependencies.

endpoints.description: Create or update an item with optional TTL seconds

sidecar_specification.purpose: In-memory data store with TTL for ephemeral persistence

agent_usage.when_to_use: When downstream modules need lightweight structured storage without database setup
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: When data must survive process restarts or requires relational queries

common_calls.description: Store a computed result with 300s TTL

failure_modes.symptom: GET returns 404 for previously stored item

failure_modes.recovery: Increase Node heap or reduce TTLs

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use a Map or plain object for the store. Implement a setInterval loop that scans for expired TTL entries every 60 seconds. Do NOT use Redis, SQLite, or any external store.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: JavaScript object keys are strings. Ensure numeric IDs are handled consistently.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Create a DataProvider class with get(id), set(id, data, ttl), delete(id). Export as a Fastify/Express route handler.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do NOT add authentication or rate limiting — that belongs in a gateway module.
